PARANOIA 
Well be publishing a whole slew of adven- tutes for use with 2nd edition Paranoia, but asthe Ist edition adventures can be dsed with 2nd edition rules and there are already a ‘whole bunch of 1st edition adventures on the ‘market, you might as well take advantage of them, ‘Most publishers’ adventures are filled with ‘game mechanics — they call for skill rolls, fevery thied line, or discuss hit points or armor classes every paragraph, That's antithetical 0 the spirit of Paranoia; most of our adventures, provide lots of ""Catch-22”" situations fram ‘which there’s no hope of escaping, lots of {equipment which malfunctions in weird Ways, lots of deranged non-player characters, and bizaure plots. We keep the mechanics (0 
That's why you can use adventures origin: ally designed for use with the Ist edition even if you're using the rules from the 2nd edition, Since rules¢ype stuf isn't mentioned much in four adventures, the changes in the rules don’t, requite that many changes in the adventures, Even though we keep the mechanics t0 a ‘minimum, though, we can’t avoid them en: tirely. Here's what needs to be changed 0 make 1st edition adventures work with the 2nd edition rules 
Percentages: Sometimes an adventure will say something like “there’s a 40% chance that Skwee-G will buy the excuse,” or "Gone R's ‘chance of hitting is 325%". The old edition us- ‘ed percentile rolls instead of 20sided dice To convert from a percentage number to a 20.sided die roll, just divide by 5, and round io the nearest whole number. 40 divided by 5 is 8, so Skwee-G buys the excuse if you roll an B or lexs on the dies 32 divided by 5 and rounded! clown is 6, so Gone-R’s weapon skill is6 
Attribute Rolls: Atribute rolls used to use a different system. If an adventure say make this ‘make this modify the kind of kind of characters tribute attribute roll or roll 
extremely easy 110 easy 2010 dificult 3010 very dificult 4010 outrageous 510 
and then make an attribute roll against the ‘modified attribute number. Example: The adventure says “make an easy attribute roll” for "make a 2010 attribute rll.” Instead, just roll against the character’s unmodified at tribute to see whether he succeeds oF not 
Checks: Adventures sometimes say “make an attribute check" or "make a skill check.” 
‘Check’ is just the old term for “roll.” Make an attute or skill roll instead. 
Armor and Laser Barrel Colors: We used to have a complex system whereby armor and barrels came in multiple colors, So you may see something like “1 ROY reflec armor.” That ‘means the armor was supposed to reflect Red, ‘Orange and Yellow laser fire, but not higher levels, Well, gosh — thats the same thing that Yellow reflec does in the new version of the game, So just look at the rightmost color in the description of reflec armor or laser barrels in this case, “V" for Yellow. That's the ac tual color of the iter, 
Pregenerated Characters 
‘Okay, that’s all you need to understand 0 hhandle all of the problems that arise in the main text of an adventure. However, in the ‘middle of each adventure, you'l find a pull ‘out printed with pregenerated player characters, Almost everything printed on these sheets is mechanics stuf, So alot of it needs to be modified ‘Modifying PCs isa bit of a pain. We explain how to modify them below, but doing so does require a considerable knowledge of the dif- ferences between Ist_and 2nd edition Paranoia. If modifying PCs strikes you as too ‘much of a bother, here’ a suggestion: cut out, teach PC's “Background,” "Current Secret Society Mission,” and other background stl, and the “Personal Equipment.” Hand these toa player, and tell him to generate his own, character, according to the rules, using the secret society mentioned in his “Current Secret Society Mission”, That way, you get to, tse the neat part of each character, without the bother of tanslating stats But suppose you want to translate the stats 
I Secondary Attributes ‘These include what we now call carrying. capacity, damage and macho bonuses, and skill bases. ‘Carrying capacity and damage and macho bonuses work the same way in both editions ff the game. No change needed. However, you'll find things like “melee bonus," “aimed weapon bonus’ and so on listed. These have been superseded by the skill bbases. Ignore them. Either calculate skill bases for each character and write them on the sheet, or be willing to look up a character's skill base when you need it 
Now we get to the tricky part. Skills have 
7. Using First Edition Adventures 
== with secant Edition Rules changed considerably from the frst edition to the second, ‘The actual skill names themselves are essen- tially unchanged. However, the first edition had a complex "skill tree” system according to which you started witha litle broad overall knowledge of a topic, and then specialized to more specific skills as you got better. We thought it was kind of neat, but it was com plex and hard to explain, and we gota lot of complaints about it, So we killed it. But in the skills section of Ist edition characters, you'll find these funny trees, Look at the bottom skill names on each tee; those are the actual skills possessed by the character For example: 
Basics (1) 
' 
‘Aimed Weapons (2) 
' 
Laser (3)

Engineering (2) 
i} 
Communications (3) 
The actual skills the character possesses are laser, fast talk, and communications engi: neering. There are two ways to handle skills from 1st edition characters * The Complicated and Time Consuming But, Eminently Fair Way: Calculate the character's skill bases from his attributes. Look at the bot tom skill names on the skill tree; find the ‘number next to each skill name. Double this number and add ito the skill base, Example: Let’s suppose a character with the skill ree above has a manual dexterity of 10, a chutz= pah of 15, anda mechanical aptitude or 2. His Skill bases for those three attributes would be 2, 4 and 0 respectively. Laser weapons is a ‘manual dexterity skill, and the number printed rnext to the skill name on the skill tree is 3. 3 doubled is 6; plus the manual dexterity kill, base of 2 is 8; so the character’ laser weapons skill is 8. By similar calculations, his fast talk skill is 10, and his communications engineer ing skill is 6
